prose:
Method / provenance

• Property computed: frontier-orbital energies (HOMO, LUMO) and the HOMO–LUMO gap of TEMPO; headline quantity reported below is the orbital gap.

• Level of theory: DFT, tier ‘standard’ (B3LYP/def2‐TZVP, RI) in PySCF.

• Geometry provenance: single-point calculation on the user-supplied XYZ file (/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/tempo/tempo.xyz); no geometry optimisation performed (notation: DFT//input-XYZ).

• Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation).

• Charge = 0; multiplicity = 2 (doublet, UKS treatment).

• Software / version: chemkit frontend with PySCF backend (version as in current chemkit deployment).

• Convergence & caveats: SCF converged in 12 cycles; no warnings were emitted by the engine (warnings array empty).
  Integrity check: status ok, trustworthy = true.

• Key numerical results (energies in eV)
  – HOMO  = −6.3375439
  – LUMO  = −2.4651235
  – HOMO–LUMO gap = 3.8724203  ← headline value
  Koopmans vertical IP = 6.3375439; EA = 2.4651235.

Reproducibility

Command executed (from result JSON):
  /home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py frontier --method dft --tier standard --charge 0 --multiplicity 2 /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/tempo/tempo.xyz --out /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmp7c60you8/live.json

The engine’s stdout/stderr was streamed live to a log file named in the pattern `frontier_orbitals_<timestamp>.out` in the working directory; this path was announced when the run began and can be inspected (e.g. via `tail -f`). The parsed JSON result resides at `/home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmp7c60you8/live.json`.

No warnings were reported by the engine.
